business_problem: Hearst's diversification into 360 different businesses worldwide—newspapers, 300+ magazines, cable TV stakes, 33 TV stations, marketing and healthcare services, and Fitch Ratings—left it with disconnected, highly customized 25-year-old finance and HR applications yielding inconsistent data and making it hard for finance and HR to adapt to business changes.
solution_summary: Hearst Communications, operating 360 distinct businesses worldwide (newspapers, 300+ magazines, TV networks, digital services, Fitch Ratings), consolidated fragmented finance and HR processes from 150 countries onto a unified Oracle Cloud suite. The integration of Oracle Cloud ERP, EPM, HCM, SCM, and LMS across all business units reduced 15 unique charts of accounts to one, enabled real-time financial reporting across diverse business segments, improved HR productivity for 20K employees, and reduced job codes from 7K to 1.7K.
business_outcome: Hearst improved financial reporting, gained near-real-time integrated finance and HR insights, reduced IT support costs, consolidated 15+ charts of accounts to one, increased HR/employee productivity for 20,000 employees, simplified internal/external audits across eight business units in 150 countries, reduced employee attrition, and replaced six discrete planning tools with one.
metrics: Reduced from 15+ unique charts of accounts to 1
Standardized processes across 8 business units in 150 countries
architecture_clues: Oracle Integration with prebuilt adapters to Salesforce, Oracle E-Business Suite, and custom SOA apps
Senior Oracle product-executive engagement on implementation steering committee with biweekly cadence
Accenture led implementation across multiple back-office applications
evidence_quotes: Hearst's implementation of Oracle Cloud applications allowed it to improve financial reporting and gain insights in near real time while reducing IT support costs.
Hearst reduced its number of job codes from 7,000 to 1,700.
Oracle Cloud HCM helped improve productivity for 20,000 employees with an intuitive and engaging experience.

solution_components.name: Oracle Cloud ERP
solution_components.role: Unified finance, procurement, and project management platform consolidating operations of 360 diverse businesses across 150 countries.

solution_components.name: Oracle Cloud EPM
solution_components.role: Enterprise planning and financial close; replaced six discrete planning software tools with single integrated system.

solution_components.name: Oracle Cloud HCM
solution_components.role: Global HR, talent management, and learning management for 20K employees across media, cable, and digital services; automation reduced new-hire onboarding cycle.

solution_components.name: Oracle Cloud SCM
solution_components.role: Supply chain and inventory management across media production and distribution.

solution_components.name: Oracle Integration
solution_components.role: Connects Oracle Cloud applications with legacy systems (E-Business Suite, Salesforce) and third-party applications; enabled 25-year legacy transition with minimal cutover risk.

data_flow: Finance data from 360 global businesses flows into unified ERP chart of accounts; HR and payroll data from 20K employees feeds into Cloud HCM; planning and close data processed through EPM; legacy systems (Salesforce, E-Business Suite, custom SOA apps) connected via Oracle Integration.
